name: KAMA MACD Adaptive Momentum v1
edge_hypothesis: KAMA (proven adaptive edge, KAMA Stoch v1 PF=1.857) adapts its smoothing speed to market noise — fast in trending, slow in ranging. MACD histogram zero-cross (proven in 9 ACCEPTs, strongest timing signal) confirms momentum direction shifts. KAMA + MACD has NEVER been tested. The hypothesis: KAMA's noise-adaptive baseline should REDUCE false entries in ranging markets (where fixed EMA generates whipsaws) while MACDh's proven momentum timing catches the actual transition. If KAMA adds adaptive edge over static EMA as a baseline, this spec should outperform EMA+MACD (PF=1.712). The test is direct: same confirmation signal (MACDh), different baseline math (adaptive vs fixed). Any PF improvement isolates the value of KAMA's adaptivity.

variants.name: kama_macdh_v1_8to1
variants.description: KAMA adaptive baseline + MACDh zero-cross timing. 8:1 R:R. Tests KAMA vs EMA as baseline for proven MACDh signal.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (12.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 12.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: Kaufman Adaptive MA. Fast in trends (period ~2), slow in noise (period ~30). Adaptive baseline replaces static EMA.
MACD histogram zero-cross. Proven in 9 ACCEPTs as strongest momentum timing signal.

variants.name: kama_macdh_v1_6to1
variants.description: KAMA + MACDh with tighter TP for higher win rate. 6:1 R:R. Tests if smaller targets improve consistency.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (9.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 9.0x ATR_14 from entry.

indicator_roles.baseline: KAMA_10_2_30 (Kaufman Adaptive MA — self-adjusting smoothing speed based on market noise ratio)
indicator_roles.confirmation: MACDh_12_26_9 (MACD histogram zero-cross — proven strongest momentum timing signal, 9 ACCEPTs)
indicator_roles.volume_volatility: ATR_14 for position sizing and stop/TP calculation
